EntryLevel.description: Basic processor suitable for everyday tasks like calling, messaging, watching videos, and browsing.
EntryLevel.example: When opening a heavy game like BGMI, the game may take longer to start and may lag while playing.

MidRange.description: Balanced processor that can handle daily tasks and some gaming smoothly.
MidRange.example: When opening a heavy game like BGMI, the game opens reasonably fast and runs smoothly most of the time.

Flagship.description: Very powerful processor designed for fast performance and heavy usage.
Flagship.example: When opening a heavy game like BGMI, the game starts instantly and runs smoothly even at high graphics settings.

4GB.description: Suitable for basic usage like calls, messaging, and light apps.
4GB.example: When switching between YouTube, WhatsApp, and Chrome, some apps may reload because the phone cannot keep many apps open.

6GB.description: Balanced RAM suitable for most users and moderate multitasking.
6GB.example: When switching between YouTube, WhatsApp, and Chrome, most apps stay open and switch smoothly.

8GB_or_more.description: Large RAM suitable for gaming and heavy multitasking.
8GB_or_more.example: When switching between YouTube, WhatsApp, Chrome, and games, apps open instantly without restarting.

64GB.description: Basic storage suitable for limited apps and photos.
64GB.example: When saving photos during a vacation trip, the storage may fill up faster if you take many photos and videos.

128GB.description: Balanced storage suitable for most users with many apps and photos.
128GB.example: When saving vacation photos and videos, you will usually have enough space without worrying about storage.

256GB_or_more.description: Large storage suitable for people who store many photos, videos, and games.
256GB_or_more.example: When recording lots of videos during a vacation trip, you will still have plenty of storage left.

LCD.description: Basic display commonly used in budget phones. Colors and blacks are normal but not very deep.
LCD.example: When watching a space scene in a movie, the sky may look dark gray instead of completely black.

OLED.description: Better display with improved colors and darker blacks compared to LCD.
OLED.example: When watching a space scene in a movie, the sky looks darker and stars stand out more clearly.

AMOLED.description: Advanced display with richer colors and deeper blacks used in many premium phones.
AMOLED.example: When watching a space scene in a movie, the sky looks completely black and the stars appear very bright.

60Hz.description: Standard refresh rate used in many phones.
60Hz.example: When scrolling Instagram, the feed moves normally but not very smoothly.

90Hz.description: Smoother display compared to standard screens.
90Hz.example: When scrolling Instagram, the movement feels smoother and easier on the eyes.

120Hz.description: Very smooth display ideal for gaming and fast scrolling.
120Hz.example: When scrolling Instagram, the feed moves extremely smoothly with almost no visible stutter.

Standard.description: Regular camera used for everyday photos.
Standard.example: When taking a group photo, you may need to step back to fit everyone into the frame.

UltraWide.description: Camera that captures a wider area in one photo.
UltraWide.example: When taking a group photo, everyone can easily fit into the frame without stepping back.

Telephoto.description: Camera used for zooming into far objects while keeping the image clear.
Telephoto.example: When taking a photo of a building far away, the zoomed image still looks clear.

4000mAh.description: Average battery capacity suitable for light usage.
4000mAh.example: When using the phone for calls, social media, and videos, the battery may last until evening.

5000mAh.description: Balanced battery capacity that usually lasts a full day.
5000mAh.example: When using the phone throughout the day for calls, videos, and social media, it usually lasts until night.

6000mAh.description: Large battery designed for heavy usage.
6000mAh.example: When using the phone heavily for gaming and videos, the battery can last more than a day.

NormalCharging.description: Basic charging speed that takes longer to fully charge the phone.
NormalCharging.example: When charging the phone in the morning, it may take about two hours to reach full battery.

FastCharging.description: Faster charging that reduces waiting time.
FastCharging.example: When charging the phone in the morning, it may reach full battery in about one hour.

SuperFastCharging.description: Very fast charging technology used in many premium phones.
SuperFastCharging.example: When charging the phone before leaving home, it may reach full battery in around thirty minutes.

Plastic.description: Lightweight material commonly used in budget phones.
Plastic.example: When holding the phone, it feels light but not very premium.

Glass.description: Premium-looking material that gives the phone a stylish appearance.
Glass.example: When holding the phone, it looks shiny and feels more premium.

MetalFrame.description: Strong and durable material used in many premium phones.
MetalFrame.example: When holding the phone, it feels solid and sturdy.

Basic.description: Entry-level processors are good for simple work like browsing, watching videos, and using Office apps.
Basic.example: When opening many Chrome tabs and a PowerPoint file together, the laptop may become slow.

MidRange.description: Mid-range processors handle office work, programming, and light gaming smoothly.
MidRange.example: When opening Chrome, Excel, and a video call at the same time, the laptop runs smoothly without slowing down.

HighEnd.description: High-end processors are very powerful and handle heavy tasks like gaming, video editing, and development easily.
HighEnd.example: When editing a 4K video or running heavy software, the laptop stays fast and responsive.

8GB.description: Suitable for basic work and everyday tasks.
8GB.example: When running a browser, Word, and a video call, the laptop works fine but may slow if many apps are open.

16GB.description: Great for multitasking and professional work.
16GB.example: When running multiple apps like Chrome, Excel, Zoom, and coding tools, everything runs smoothly.

32GB_or_more.description: Designed for heavy professional tasks like video editing and 3D design.
32GB_or_more.example: When editing large videos or running design software, the laptop still runs smoothly.

HDD.description: Traditional storage that provides large capacity but slower speed.
HDD.example: When turning on the laptop or opening apps, it may take noticeable time.

SSD.description: Modern storage that makes the laptop much faster.
SSD.example: When turning on the laptop, it starts within a few seconds and apps open quickly.

LED.description: Standard TV display used in many affordable TVs.
LED.example: When watching a night scene in a movie, dark areas may look slightly gray.

QLED.description: Improved LED display with brighter colors.
QLED.example: When watching a nature documentary, colors like green forests and blue oceans look brighter.

OLED.description: Premium display with very deep blacks and excellent picture quality.
OLED.example: When watching a space movie scene, the sky looks completely black and stars look very bright.

FullHD.description: Basic TV resolution suitable for smaller TVs.
FullHD.example: When watching regular TV channels, the picture looks clear enough.

4K.description: Very sharp picture with much more detail.
4K.example: When watching a 4K movie, you can see very fine details like textures in landscapes.

SingleDoor.description: Compact refrigerator suitable for small families or single users.
SingleDoor.example: When storing groceries for a few days, it provides enough space but may feel small for large families.

DoubleDoor.description: Separate freezer and fridge compartments with more storage.
DoubleDoor.example: When storing vegetables, fruits, and frozen food together, everything fits comfortably.

SideBySide.description: Large refrigerator with lots of storage space and modern design.
SideBySide.example: When storing groceries for a big family or parties, there is plenty of space.

DirectCool.description: Basic cooling system that requires manual defrosting.
DirectCool.example: Ice can build up in the freezer and may need to be cleaned occasionally.

FrostFree.description: Advanced cooling that prevents ice buildup automatically.
FrostFree.example: You never need to manually remove ice from the freezer.

SemiAutomatic.description: Budget washing machine where some steps require manual work.
SemiAutomatic.example: After washing clothes, you may need to move them manually to the dryer section.

TopLoad.description: Fully automatic washing machine with lid on top.
TopLoad.example: You just add clothes and detergent, and the machine handles washing and drying automatically.

FrontLoad.description: Premium washing machine known for better cleaning and water efficiency.
FrontLoad.example: When washing dirty clothes, the machine cleans thoroughly and uses less water.

WindowAC.description: Basic air conditioner installed in a window or wall.
WindowAC.example: When cooling a small bedroom, it cools the room but can be slightly noisy.

SplitAC.description: Modern AC with indoor and outdoor units, quieter and more efficient.
SplitAC.example: When cooling a bedroom, the room becomes cool quickly and quietly.

NonInverter.description: Traditional AC compressor that turns on and off repeatedly.
NonInverter.example: When the room becomes cool, the AC stops and then starts again later.

Inverter.description: Advanced compressor that adjusts power automatically to save electricity.
Inverter.example: When the room reaches the desired temperature, the AC slows down instead of turning off, saving energy.
